title: begonia marinae
intro: Begonia marinae, also known as the sea begonia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Begoniaceae that grows in coastal areas. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. It has been found to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating skin conditions, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory issues. The herbal preparations of Begonia marinae typically involve drying the leaves and stems to make teas, infusions, or capsules, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential uses and effects.
